title: Bridge Essay: Dissociating meditation proficiency and experience dependent EEG changes during

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Formless/Absorption** (Phase 8) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Formless/Absorption to mechanistic neuroscience.

**Dissociating meditation proficiency and experience dependent EEG changes during traditional Vipassana meditation practice.** — Biol Psychol. PMID: 29526764. DOI: 10.1016/j.biopsycho.2018.03.004. Meditation, as taught by most schools of practice, consists of aset of heterogeneous techniques. We wanted to assess if EEG profiles varied across different meditation techniques, proficiency levels and experience of the practitioners. We examined EEG dynamics in Vipassana meditators (Novice, Senior meditators and Teachers) while they engaged in their traditional meditation practice (concentration, mindfulness and loving kindness in astructured manner) as taught by S.N. Goenka. Seniors and Tea
